data.title: BlockFin replaces Baker Tilly with Suya WWC as auditor amid cost-cutting drive
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

The latest update is out from Bison Finance Group Limited ( (HK:0888) ).

BlockFin Holdings Limited has replaced its long-time auditor, Baker Tilly Hong Kong Limited, after failing to agree on the audit and review fees for its 2026 financial statements and interim report, amid ongoing efforts to curb operating costs. Despite Baker Tilly’s earlier disclaimer of opinion on the 2025 accounts, both the board and audit committee say there are no unresolved disputes and expect no significant impact on the 2026 audit timetable.

Following Baker Tilly’s resignation, BlockFin’s board, on the recommendation of its audit committee, appointed Suya WWC CPA Limited as the new auditor with effect from 24 July 2026, to serve until the next annual general meeting. The move follows a fee benchmarking exercise in which rival firms, including Suya, quoted materially lower fees than Baker Tilly, underscoring the company’s focus on aligning audit costs with its downsized operations and broader cost-control strategy.

More about Bison Finance Group Limited

BlockFin Holdings Limited, formerly known as Bison Finance Group Limited, is a Hong Kong-listed company incorporated in Bermuda and traded on the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong. The group has recently reduced the scale of its operations and is implementing cost-control measures, including tighter management of administrative expenses, to improve liquidity and support its ability to continue as a going concern.

Average Trading Volume: 245,891

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Sell

Current Market Cap: HK$55.12M

data.title: International Genius faces extended trading halt after secretary resignation triggers new HKEX demands
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

International Genius Company ( (HK:0033) ) has provided an update.

International Genius Company has disclosed that it is no longer compliant with Hong Kong Listing Rule 3.28 after the resignation of its company secretary on 15 June 2026, leaving the firm without a qualified officer in that role. The Stock Exchange has issued additional resumption guidance, requiring the company to restore compliance and address the substantive issues behind its trading suspension before any relisting of its shares can be approved.

Trading in the company’s shares, suspended since 2 October 2025, will remain halted until the group satisfies both the original and new resumption conditions and publishes its audited 2025 annual results. The company has pledged to provide further announcements and quarterly updates on its progress, while warning shareholders and potential investors to exercise caution amid ongoing regulatory uncertainty and delayed financial disclosure.

More about International Genius Company

International Genius Company is a Hong Kong-listed firm incorporated in the Cayman Islands, trading under stock code 33. The company operates through a group structure, with an executive-led board and independent non-executive directors, and is subject to Hong Kong’s Listing Rules and securities regulations governing its disclosure and governance practices.

The board currently comprises two executive directors and three independent non-executive directors, reflecting a typical governance framework for mid-sized listed companies in the region. Its compliance obligations include maintaining qualified key officers, timely financial reporting and adherence to resumption and suspension rules set by the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Sell

Current Market Cap: HK$223.3M

Shanghai Seer Intelligent Technology Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:6106) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Shanghai Seer Intelligent Technology has called an extraordinary general meeting to be held in Shanghai on 13 August 2026 to seek shareholder approval for a broad mandate to repurchase its H shares. The proposed mandate would allow the board, and the chairman by delegation, to repurchase up to 10% of the company’s issued share capital, excluding treasury shares, subject to compliance with PRC laws, Hong Kong listing rules and other regulatory requirements.

If approved, the board would gain extensive authority to design and implement a detailed buyback plan, including setting prices, volumes, timing and duration of repurchases. The resolution also contemplates flexibility to cancel repurchased shares, reduce registered capital or hold them as treasury shares based on market conditions and capital management needs, underscoring a proactive approach to balance sheet optimisation and potential enhancement of shareholder value.

Leader Education Limited ( (HK:1449) ) has shared an update.

Leader Education Limited has entered into a placing agreement to issue up to 133,333,000 new shares under its general mandate at HK$0.265 per share, representing about 20% of its existing share capital. data.title: Hanking Gold Wins Key Approvals to Launch Mt Bundy Gold Project Development
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

An announcement from China Hanking Holdings Ltd. ( (HK:3788) ) is now available.

Hanking Gold International Limited’s subsidiary Primary Gold Pty Ltd has obtained a site clearance permit for the Mt Bundy Gold Project processing plant from the Department of Lands, Planning and Environment and a Notice of Authority to Commence Mining Activity from the Department of Mining and Energy in Australia’s Northern Territory. These approvals, granted under relevant environmental and mineral titles legislation, cover a project with 3.47 Moz of gold resources and 1.88 Moz of gold reserves and build on earlier environmental approvals for both underground and open-pit mining, as well as processing infrastructure.

Following the permits, the company has begun site clearance work, invited qualified mining tenders and is preparing detailed tendering documents, positioning the project to move into construction. Management indicated that processing plant construction is targeted to start in the third quarter of 2026 with gold production planned for the first quarter of 2028, marking a significant operational milestone that could enhance Hanking Gold’s production profile and long-term growth prospects, while the company cautioned shareholders and potential investors to be prudent when dealing in its shares.

More about China Hanking Holdings Ltd.

Hanking Gold International Limited is a gold mining company incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed in Hong Kong. Through its wholly owned subsidiary Primary Gold Pty Ltd, the company focuses on developing the Mt Bundy Gold Project in Australia’s Northern Territory, which holds significant gold resources and reserves under the JORC (2012) Code.

data.title: Hanking Gold Issues Profit Warning as Iron and FX Headwinds Push First-Half into Loss
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

China Hanking Holdings Ltd. ( (HK:3788) ) has issued an announcement.

Hanking Gold International Limited has warned investors that it expects to swing to a loss of about RMB45 million to RMB60 million for the first half of 2026, compared with a profit of RMB105 million a year earlier. The reversal is mainly attributed to lower production and sales in its domestic iron ore and high-purity iron operations, which cut net profit by roughly RMB70 million.

Additional pressure came from a sharp rise in option amortisation expenses in its Australian gold business, which climbed to about RMB32 million, and foreign exchange losses of around RMB27 million due to RMB fluctuations against the US and Hong Kong dollars. The company reiterated that it is prioritising development of its gold business and cautioned shareholders and potential investors to be prudent ahead of its interim results release in late August 2026.

More about China Hanking Holdings Ltd.

Hanking Gold International Limited is a Hong Kong-listed resources company engaged in iron ore, high-purity iron and gold businesses, with operations spanning domestic iron production and an Australian gold segment. The group focuses on developing its gold business in line with established strategies while maintaining its iron-related operations in China.

Dashan Education Holdings Limited ( (HK:9986) ) has provided an announcement.

Dashan Education Holdings has disclosed that its appeal against a prior Stock Exchange decision to cancel its listing will be heard by the Listing Review Committee on 19 August 2026. The company stresses that this review will be conducted de novo, with the committee reconsidering all evidence and arguments afresh in what is effectively its final chance under the listing rules to overturn the delisting move.

The board underscores that if the Listing Review Committee upholds the earlier decision, Dashan’s shares will be delisted from the Hong Kong market, leaving only the possibility of seeking judicial review outside the exchange framework. data.title: XtalPi Flags Interim Loss as R&D Push Weighs on Earnings
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

The latest announcement is out from QuantumPharm, Inc. ( (HK:2228) ).

XtalPi Holdings Limited has warned investors that it expects to swing to a net loss of about RMB215 million to RMB275 million for the six months ended June 30, 2026, compared with a net profit of roughly RMB82.8 million a year earlier. The company also anticipates a decline in headline revenue to around RMB380 million to RMB400 million, largely due to a high comparison base created by a major pipeline licensing upfront payment booked in the prior period.

Excluding that one-off licensing effect, XtalPi projects at least RMB250 million in revenue, more than 65% higher year-on-year, with its AI for Science intelligent solutions set to grow over 120% to no less than RMB180 million. The group attributes the loss to a more than 50% increase in research and development spending to at least RMB340 million, as it accelerates investment in autonomous labs, agentic systems and diversified drug technology platforms, a strategy it believes will reinforce long-term R&D strength and sustain future revenue growth.

More about QuantumPharm, Inc.

XtalPi Holdings Limited operates in the AI-driven drug discovery and scientific research sector, providing intelligent solutions, robotic laboratory systems and related services to pharmaceutical and biotech clients. The group focuses on Scientific AI, Physical AI and agentic systems, and is expanding into proprietary drug pipelines and multimodal technology platforms spanning small and large molecules, molecular glues, peptides and oligonucleotides.

The company is pursuing a strategic shift from pure service provision toward building asset-based platforms and pipelines with long-term commercialization potential. It has launched XtalPi Science, an open intelligent R&D platform aimed at scientific research scenarios, and is investing heavily in autonomous laboratories and integrated AI systems to create a closed-loop research and development ecosystem.

Average Trading Volume: 58,873,850

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Majestic Dragon Aerotech Holdings Limited plans to overhaul its constitutional documents by replacing its existing amended and restated bye-laws with a new set that aligns more closely with updated Hong Kong listing regulations. The board will seek shareholder approval for the new bye-laws by special resolution at the annual general meeting scheduled for 18 August 2026, with a circular detailing the amendments to be dispatched in due course. Qilu Expressway Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:1576) ) has provided an announcement.

Qilu Expressway Company Limited, a PRC-incorporated toll-road operator listed in Hong Kong, generates the bulk of its turnover from toll income on expressways in Shandong. Its asset-heavy balance sheet and reliance on regional traffic patterns underscore its role as a long-term infrastructure provider in China’s transport sector.

For the six months ended 30 June 2026, the Group reported revenue of about RMB1,113 million, up 4.25% year-on-year, while net profit slipped 1.79% to roughly RMB195 million as toll income fell 4.12% amid traffic diversion and construction-related closures.

Total non-current assets edged down 1.42% and equity declined 1.06% over the period, while current assets rose 14.8%, indicating some strengthening of short-term liquidity even as profitability softened due to operational headwinds on key road sections.

Its business performance is closely tied to traffic volumes, the configuration of surrounding road networks and construction activity, making it sensitive to diversion and closure effects on key routes.

The company’s capital structure includes substantial non-current assets and liabilities typical of long-term infrastructure operations, with equity reflecting its accumulated investments and earnings in the toll-road segment.

data.title: AsiaInfo warns of wider first-half loss as it doubles down on AI strategy
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

AsiaInfo Technologies Limited ( (HK:1675) ) has provided an update.

AsiaInfo Technologies warned that revenue for the first half of 2026 is expected to fall to about RMB2.10 billion–RMB2.30 billion from RMB2.598 billion a year earlier, with net loss widening to roughly RMB460 million–RMB490 million. The downturn stems from weaker telecom core system demand, deliberate cuts to low-quality ICT and labor-intensive projects, and one-off severance costs from workforce restructuring.

Excluding restructuring charges, the group still anticipates a larger underlying loss of RMB240 million–RMB270 million as it reshapes its portfolio. Management says the short-term hit reflects an “AI First” strategy, with faster growth in smart digital operations, progress toward commercialising its Physical AI products for manufacturers, and deployment of a full-stack satellite internet platform integrated into China’s low-Earth orbit constellation, positioning the company for longer-term AI-era growth.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1675) stock is a Hold

Shenzhen Pagoda Industrial (Group) Corp. Ltd. Class H ( (HK:2411) ) has provided an announcement.

Shenzhen Pagoda Industrial (Group) Corporation Limited expects a significant turnaround for the first half of 2026, forecasting net profit of at least RMB20 million versus a loss of about RMB342 million a year earlier. The company also anticipates revenue growth of no less than 7% over the same period, signaling a strong recovery in its core retail and supply chain operations.

Management attributes the improvement to AI-driven systems for intelligent ordering and store diagnostics, a net addition of 292 fruit retail stores, optimized product mix, and tighter cost control. The group is also monetizing its long-built fruit supply chain capabilities by serving external customers and rolling out new store formats under seven local brands, which are providing additional momentum for revenue and profit growth and potentially strengthening its competitive position in the fresh food retail sector.

More about Shenzhen Pagoda Industrial (Group) Corp. Ltd. Class H

Shenzhen Pagoda Industrial (Group) Corporation Limited is a China-based joint stock company focused on fruit retail and related fresh food products. Leveraging more than two decades of experience in the fruit supply chain, the group operates a large network of specialized fruit stores and increasingly offers supply chain services to third parties, while exploring new formats such as “fruit + snacks” outlets and community fresh food stores.

data.title: Luk Fook Sets August 2026 AGM to Approve Results, Dividend and New Share Mandate
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Luk Fook Holdings (International) ( (HK:0590) ) has provided an update.

Luk Fook Holdings (International) Limited has convened its next annual general meeting for 20 August 2026 in Hong Kong, where shareholders will review the audited consolidated financial statements and directors’ and auditor’s reports for the year ended 31 March 2026. The meeting will also consider a proposed final dividend, the re‑election of retiring directors with authority for the board to set their remuneration, and the re‑appointment of PricewaterhouseCoopers as auditor.

Shareholders will further vote on a general mandate authorising the board to allot, issue and deal with additional shares up to 10% of the company’s issued share capital, excluding treasury shares, subject to defined limits and timeframe. Approval of this mandate would give the company added flexibility in future capital raising or share‑based arrangements, potentially supporting strategic expansion while diluting existing holdings only within a capped threshold.
